## Origin And Design Goals

Explain who created the language, why it exists, and which constraints shaped
the design. Cite official history, release, specification, or governance
sources when the details are not obvious from the official site.

## Best-Fit Use Cases

Describe where the language is a strong practical fit. Frame the judgment in
terms of runtime target, ecosystem maturity, team skill, deployment model, and
maintenance cost.

## Poor-Fit Or Risky Use Cases

Describe contexts where the language may impose avoidable cost or risk. Avoid
universal claims unless the source support is unusually strong.

## Language Highlights

- Distinguishing feature or tradeoff.
- Distinguishing feature or tradeoff.
- Distinguishing feature or tradeoff.

## Syntax Example

```text
small runnable or clearly illustrative example
```

## Tooling And Ecosystem

Summarize package management, build tools, editor support, deployment shape,
and ecosystem maturity.

## Governance And Implementation

Explain standardization, project governance, primary implementation, and release
process when known.
